Start Operating. Think Like an Analyst.

// The Problem

You've Studied.
You're Still Not Ready.


You've burned through video courses, certification prep books, and YouTube tutorials. You can define terms. You can pass a multiple-choice exam. But when someone asks you to triage a real alert, analyze a log, or build an incident timeline you freeze.

That's not your fault. That's by design. Most cybersecurity training was built to get you certified, not competent. There's a difference.

The industry keeps hiring people with letters after their name who've never made a real analytic call under pressure. Employers are tired of it. And so are you.

  • [ERR-01]
    Courses built for test day, not the job. You pass the exam. You show up on day one. You have no idea how to work a real SOC queue or interpret what you're actually looking at.
  • [ERR-02]
    No experience, no hires. Entry-level postings demand 2 3 years of experience. You can't get experience without a job. The system is rigged against you unless you can prove capability another way.
  • [ERR-03]
    Theory without decision-making. You know what MITRE ATT&CK is. But can you use it to attribute behavior, scope a threat, or write an intelligence assessment? There's a gap between knowing and doing.
  • [ERR-04]
    Passive consumption masquerading as training. Watching videos feels productive. It isn't. Analytic skill is forged through decisions, not observation.
// The Solution

Simulation-Based Analyst Training. Not Another Course.


TRCIA I was built from operational analyst principles the same analytic discipline used in military and national security contexts and applied directly to cybersecurity operations.

Every module drops you into a live simulation: a real SOC queue with active alerts, log files full of noise and signal, timeline reconstructions you have to build, and adversary behavior you have to track. You make decisions. You get feedback. You level up.

This isn't passive learning. You don't just consume information. You operate and the program holds you to a standard.

// Core Training Philosophy
  • Every module includes an interactive simulation not a quiz
  • You advance by meeting performance standards, not just completing content
  • Analyst thinking frameworks built from analyst tradecraft
  • MITRE ATT&CK integrated into real threat analysis workflows
  • SOC + CTI workflows taught in parallel, not in isolation
  • Capstone mission replicates a full-scope incident start to report
  • Aligned to NICE cybersecurity workforce framework work roles
  • No external tools required fully self-contained training environment
// Analyst Capabilities

What You Will Be Able To Do

When you complete TRCIA I, these are not things you'll have learned about. These are things you'll be able to execute.

01
Triage and Prioritize Alerts
Work a live SOC queue under realistic conditions distinguish signal from noise, assess severity, and make correct escalation calls without hand-holding.
02
Analyze Logs and Detect Anomalies
Read and interpret authentication logs, network traffic, and system events. Identify behavioral anomalies that indicate malicious activity in progress.
03
Reconstruct Incident Timelines
Build accurate, defensible timelines from fragmented evidence the core skill of every effective analyst who has ever produced a solid incident report.
04
Correlate Evidence and Build Cases
Connect indicators across multiple data sources. Identify patterns, map adversary behavior to MITRE ATT&CK TTPs, and build supported analytic assessments.
05
Analyze Adversary Behavior and OPSEC
Understand how threat actors think how they move, how they hide, and how their own operational patterns expose them to detection.
06
Write Analytic Judgments and Reports
Produce clear, structured intelligence products the kind that decision-makers can act on using the same frameworks used in professional CTI environments.
// Program Architecture

How the Program Works

Ten modules. Each one builds on the last. Each one requires performance, not just participation. You don't move forward by completing content you move forward by meeting the standard.

M01
Analyst Foundations & Operational Mindset
Operational analyst principles applied to cybersecurity. How analysts think, structure problems, and avoid common cognitive failures.
SIM: Cognitive Bias Lab
M02
SOC Operations and the Alert Lifecycle
How a SOC functions, the alert triage workflow, escalation paths, and your role within the broader security operation.
SIM: Live SOC Queue
M03
Log Analysis and Anomaly Detection
Reading and interpreting authentication events, firewall logs, system logs, and network traffic for indicators of compromise.
SIM: Log Analysis Bench
M04
Network Traffic and Protocol Analysis
Understanding normal versus suspicious network behavior, lateral movement signatures, and data exfiltration patterns.
SIM: Traffic Analysis Engine
M05
Threat Intelligence and Adversary Profiling
The CTI intelligence cycle, indicator management, threat actor profiling, and MITRE ATT&CK TTP mapping in operational context.
SIM: Adversary Profile Builder
M06
Evidence Correlation and Case Building
Connecting disparate indicators into coherent analytic pictures. Hypothesis generation, evidence weighting, and structured analysis techniques.
SIM: Evidence Board
M07
Timeline Reconstruction
Building defensible incident timelines from fragmented, out-of-sequence evidence. Temporal analysis and sequence disambiguation.
SIM: Timeline Builder
M08
Incident Response Integration
How the analyst function integrates with IR workflows containment coordination, evidence preservation, and stakeholder communication.
SIM: IR Decision Simulator
M09
Analytic Writing and Intelligence Products
Producing structured intelligence reports, executive summaries, and TTPs-in-context assessments for different audiences and classification levels.
SIM: Report Grader
M10
Pre-Capstone Integration
Full workflow integration review. Competency verification across all prior modules. Preparation for the full-scope capstone mission.
SIM: Skills Verification
Capstone Mission
Final Evaluation
A full-scope incident simulation from initial alert through complete intelligence product. You receive raw data, a partial SOC queue, fragmented logs, and network telemetry. You produce the full analytic picture triage, timeline, attribution, and final report from scratch. This is the mission. There is no partial credit for close enough.
80%
Per Module
Standard
85%
Capstone
Standard
// Simulation Environments

Inside the Training

[ SIM-TYPE // SOC-QUEUE ]
Live SOC Alert Queue
Work through a realistic SOC dashboard with active alert feeds, severity levels, and time pressure. Make triage decisions escalate, close, investigate and justify your calls. Includes realistic noise-to-signal ratios and alert fatigue dynamics.
[ SIM-TYPE // LOG-ANALYSIS ]
Log Analysis Bench
Raw authentication logs, firewall events, and Windows Event IDs presented without pre-filtering. Identify the indicators, discard the noise, and build your findings into a structured assessment. The data is dirty. That's intentional.
[ SIM-TYPE // TIMELINE ]
Timeline Reconstruction Engine
Fragments of a multi-stage incident delivered out of sequence. You reorder, timestamp, and reconstruct the attack chain from initial access through impact. Then defend your timeline with evidence attribution for each node.
[ SIM-TYPE // EVIDENCE-BOARD ]
Evidence Correlation Board
A digital link analysis environment where you connect indicators, assign confidence levels, map TTPs, and build toward an analytic judgment. Designed after the structured analysis techniques used in professional intelligence environments.
[ SIM-TYPE // HYPOTHESIS-LAB ]
Hypothesis Lab
Build competing hypotheses from the same evidence set. Apply analytic rigor ACH-style techniques to identify the most supported explanation. Learn how analysts stay objective when evidence is incomplete or contradictory.
[ SIM-TYPE // REPORT-GRADER ]
Intelligence Report Grader
Submit analytic write-ups and receive structured feedback on clarity, evidence support, analytic confidence calibration, and actionability. Writing to a standard is a skill. This module forces you to develop it.
// Credential

The TRCIA I Certification


The TacRaven Cybersecurity Intelligence Analyst I Foundational (TRCIA I) credential is not awarded for showing up. It is earned by meeting a defined performance standard across ten modules and a full-scope capstone.

It doesn't prove you watched content. It proves you made analyst-grade decisions under realistic conditions. It's evidence of capability not completion.

TRCIA I is aligned to the NICE Cybersecurity Workforce Framework across four work roles:

  • Cyber Defense Analyst (PR-CDA-001)
  • Threat/Warning Analyst (AN-TWA-001)
  • Cyber Defense Incident Responder (PR-CIR-001)
  • Law Enforcement / Digital Evidence Analyst

// Candidate Profile

Who This Is For and Who It Is Not

This Is For You If…
  • You're transitioning into cybersecurity from another field military, law enforcement, IT support, or a completely unrelated career
  • You've been studying for certifications but feel like you still couldn't do the actual job
  • You're a veteran who wants to apply analytic and mission-planning discipline to a civilian cyber career
  • You're an early-career analyst who wants to build real analyst capability before your first role or to level up in your current one
  • You want a credential that proves you can think and operate, not just that you attended a course
  • You're willing to meet a standard and you understand that rigor is the point, not the obstacle
This Is NOT For You If…
  • You're looking to collect another completion certificate without being tested on your ability to actually perform
  • You expect to watch videos on 2x speed and call it training
  • You're not willing to fail a module, be given feedback, and try again
  • You want shortcuts. This program has a standard. The standard is the product.
  • You already have significant hands-on SOC or CTI experience and are looking for advanced red team or offensive security training

Founding Cohort Access


The founding cohort represents the first group of analysts trained under the TRCIA I framework. Founding cohort members receive the lowest price this program will ever be offered at and direct input into how the program evolves.

This is not a sale. This is the ground floor. Once the founding cohort closes, pricing increases and locks at standard rate.

If you are reading this, the founding cohort is still open. That will not always be true.
